AMD.reasoning: MACRO REGIME: Mixed-to-cautious transitional regime. The Fed held at 3.5%-3.75% for the third consecutive time on April 29, cementing higher-for-longer. US-Iran military blockade of Iranian ports is the dominant left-tail risk — a Strait of Hormuz closure would spike oil, re-accelerate inflation, and force further Fed hawkishness. Mega-cap earnings split (Alphabet/Amazon beat on AI capex, Meta -9% capex shock) creates sector uncertainty. S&P April +9% is the strongest month since 2020, but the Dow logged a fifth consecutive losing session. AI hardware thematic remains intact as the primary risk-on engine, but the regime is not cleanly risk-on — it is transitional with amplifying headwinds. Critically, the wiki regime page flags AMD earnings (late April / early May) as the 'next binary read-through' for the entire AI hardware thesis. RELATIVE STRENGTH: AMD is massively outperforming the broad market — ROC 21d at +72% vs S&P approximately +9% for April. This is extreme late-cycle outperformance, not early-cycle entry. The system mandate is explicit: bullish setups should come early in a sector's relative-strength uptrend, not late. At 47% above SMA-50 and 61% above SMA-200, AMD is statistically stretched (Z-score 2.47 vs 50-period). The stock has already delivered the move; entering now is chasing, not front-running. ASYMMETRY R/R MATH: Current price ~345 (hourly). Nearest resistance: 352.99 (hourly pivot high, 2x tested, volume-confirmed) = approximately 2.3% upside. Nearest support: 328.81 (hourly pivot low, 6x tested, volume-confirmed) = approximately 4.7% downside. At immediate resistance target: 2.3% upside vs 4.7% downside = 0.5:1 — far below the 2:1 minimum. Extending to fib ext 1.272 at 366.67 requires breaking through 352.99 and assumes continued parabolic extension: 6.3% upside vs 4.7% downside = 1.3:1 — still below the 2:1 threshold. Even fib ext 1.618 at 384.07 (11.3% upside vs 4.7% to 328.81 stop) = 2.4:1, but this assumes blowout earnings and sustained AI capex narrative expansion — a binary bet, not a swing setup. CAPITAL PRESERVATION FLAGS: (1) Hourly MACD histogram turned negative (-1.69) while price is near highs — distribution signal forming on the hourly timeframe. (2) Hourly ADX: -DI (33.53) is now dominant over +DI (20.45), indicating sellers controlling short-term price action despite price still elevated. (3) Daily RSI-14 at 82.09 and RSI-21 at 85.78 — extreme overbought readings that historically precede mean-reversion pullbacks in individual names even within bull trends. (4) Bollinger %B at 0.885 — pressing the upper band on the daily. (5) Imminent earnings binary event: AMD earnings are flagged as the next macro read-through. A miss or guidance cut would collapse the setup, and options-implied volatility around earnings inflates the risk denominator. CONCLUSION: The daily trend is undeniably strong (ADX 73.57, EMA aligned uptrend, OBV rising). However, the entry timing is wrong: late-cycle sector rotation, sub-2:1 R/R at all realistic near-term targets, hourly distribution signals forming, extreme overbought daily RSI, and an imminent earnings binary all argue against initiating a new swing position here. This is a pass on R/R grounds and late-cycle timing, not a reversal call. A pullback to the 310-315 zone (prior gap-fill + 10-SMA area) with hourly MACD re-crossing bullish would create the asymmetric setup this framework requires.
